page: Amazon Web Services x2gd.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro System hypervisor, this server features 1024.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. The hardware configuration includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory-bound performance. The instance achieves a strong rating in stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and cached small-block read memory bandwidth. It also demonstrates high throughput in redis SET operations, reaching 72.65 million operations per second, and static web serving at 43.93 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a geekbench single-core score of 968.0 and a weak rating in single-threaded gzip compression. Large-block uncached memory read and write speeds fall into the average tier at 153.87 GB/sec and 74.77 GB/sec respectively.

The ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and power efficiency, translating to qualitative cost advantages for memory-intensive workloads compared to traditional x86 alternatives. However, the lack of x86-specific instruction sets like AVX-512 or AMX limits its suitability for specialized vector math or heavy on-CPU machine learning acceleration, as reflected in the weak performance for large-model LLM text generation. The presence of local NVMe SSDs provides high-speed, low-latency local storage, but users must manage the ephemeral nature of instance store volumes.

This server configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale in-memory databases like Redis, distributed caching layers, real-time analytics, and software compilation pipelines. The high memory capacity combined with dedicated physical cores also supports high-throughput web serving and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 1024.0 GB of RAM, yielding 16.0 GB per core, alongside 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance, high cached memory bandwidth, and excellent redis SET throughput at 72.65 million operations per second. Conversely, single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks are weak. The ARM-based architecture offers high memory density and qualitative cost efficiency for scaled deployments, though it lacks x86 vector instructions. This instance is suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server with 64 Graviton cores, 1024 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and redis performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server featuring 64 Graviton cores, 1024 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized platform featuring high-density memory, dedicated physical cores, and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 cores running at 2.5 GHz.
1024 GB of system memory providing 16 GB of RAM per physical core.
3800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage.
Strong multi-core performance and high-throughput redis operations.
Weak single-core performance and lack of x86 vector instructions.
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and software compilation.
